content.problemStatement:
Daniil is a royal gardener. He takes care of a garden with NNN plants numbered 111 through NNN. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the initial height of the iii-th plant is AiA_iAi‚Äã. Unfortunately, the Queen doesn't like the garden, so she asked Daniil to cut some plants ‚Äî in order to satisfy the Queen's request, the iii-th plant should have height BiB_iBi‚Äã (for each 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N).

Daniil is allowed to perform the following operation an arbitrary number of times (including zero):


Let's denote the current heights of plants by H1,H2,‚Ä¶,HNH_1, H_2, \dots, H_NH1‚Äã,H2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,HN‚Äã.
Choose two indices LLL and RRR (1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N) and a new height hhh such that h‚â§Hih \le H_ih‚â§Hi‚Äã for each iii between LLL and RRR inclusive.
Cut plants LLL through RRR down to height hhh, i.e. change the height of plant iii to hhh for each L‚â§i‚â§RL \le i \le RL‚â§i‚â§R.
Some time ago, Daniil was one of the best competitive programmers. Therefore, he is interested in the minimum number of operations needed to satisfy the Queen's request. Can you help him?
constraints: 1‚â§Ai,Bi‚â§1091 \le A_i, B_i \le 10^91‚â§Ai‚Äã,Bi‚Äã‚â§109 for each valid iii
the initial heights of all plants are identical
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN denoting the number of plants.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the initial heights of plants.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã denoting the final heights of plants.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the minimum number of operations needed to obtain the desired sequence of heights or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if it is impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:
Daniil can perform two operations: reduce the height of the first plant to 222 and reduce the height of the third plant to 222. Note that it is not possible to reduce the heights of all three plants to 222 in one operation, because the second plant's height is less than 222. We need two operations, thus the answer is 222.
Example case 2:
In the first operation, cut the plants in the interval [2,4][2, 4][2,4] down to height 222. Afterwards, cut the third plant down to height 111. Finally, cut the plants in the interval [6,7][6, 7][6,7].
Example case 3:
All plants already have the desired heights, so the answer is 000.
Example case 4:
It is impossible to achieve the desired sequence of heights.
